problem_spec.summary: Determine the guilty agent in a four-agent truth-teller system specified directly by propositional formulas, with exactly one universal liar and exactly one guilty agent.
givens: Exactly one agent is the universal liar and exactly one agent is guilty.
Every statement made by the liar is false; every statement made by each other agent is true.
Notation: Li = agent i is the liar, Ti = agent i is truthful, Gi = agent i is guilty, Pi = agent i was present at the event.
ask: Determine the guilty agent. Return a single digit from 1 to 4.

parameter(s).name: statement_count_per_agent
parameter(s).purpose: records the number of statements made by each agent

parameter(s).name: liar_culprit_coupling
parameter(s).purpose: records whether the liar and culprit must be different agents

invariant(s).name: unique_liar
invariant(s).statement: Exactly one agent is the universal liar, and every statement made by that agent is false.

essential_assumption(s): Each agent is globally truthful or globally dishonest across all of their statements.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Exhaustive world search leaves a unique guilty label after enforcing all statement truths and any referenced statement-truth variables.

technique(s).name: exhaustive_world_search
technique(s).purpose: test all candidate worlds against the statement set and keep only consistent assignments

theorem(s).name: classical_propositional_semantics
theorem(s).role: determines the truth of each statement under a candidate world

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: A finite symbolic world model can be checked exactly by exhaustive enumeration.

critical_trick(s): Represent the puzzle as a finite constraint system and search over all liar/guilty assignments until only one culprit label survives.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: enumerate liar, guilty, presence, and referenced truth-value assignments exactly

task(s).name: symbolic_world_search
task(s).reason: verify the culprit by exhaustive propositional evaluation
